summary: Phlebotomy is a perfect match for your cognitive preference for procedural, protocol-driven work that avoids complex mathematics or deductive reasoning. While the ability to leave work behind at the end of a shift supports your wellbeing, the high risk of physical burnout and chronic pain presents a significant barrier to long-term career sustainability. Success in this role will depend on your ability to manage high patient volumes and navigate less predictable hospital shifts until you can secure a more stable outpatient clinic position.
